prompt: Build a generative art piece with particles that flow through a 2D Perlin noise field. Include interactive controls (play/pause, speed slider, particle count slider) and a color palette that shifts as particles move. Make it loop infinitely and be visually satisfying. Use Canvas for rendering.

assertions.text: Uses Canvas API for high-performance particle rendering
assertions.evidence: Canvas element created and requestAnimationFrame used for 60fps animation

assertions.text: Implements Perlin or Simplex noise for particle flow direction
assertions.evidence: SimplexNoise class implemented with proper gradient interpolation and seeded randomness
Button toggles animation loop, visual feedback provided with color change

assertions.text: Speed slider adjusts particle velocity from 0.5x to 2x
assertions.evidence: Slider controls speed multiplier, real-time value display shown
Slider controls particle array size, updates in real-time

assertions.text: Color palette shifts based on particle position (hue rotation)
assertions.evidence: HSL-based coloring where hue changes based on particle x/y coordinates

assertions.text: Particles wrap at edges and loop infinitely
assertions.evidence: Particle position wraps at canvas boundaries, animation never stops
FPS counter displayed and consistently shows 55-60fps on modern hardware
